description: Equipment Lifecycle Tracking - Add real data persistence for home equipment, replacement cost estimates, aging notifications, and lifecycle insights

userStories.title: Equipment context with AsyncStorage persistence
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a context provider that stores equipment records so data persists across sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create EquipmentContext in src/context/EquipmentContext.tsx
State includes: equipment array (EquipmentItem[]), isLoaded boolean
Provides: addEquipment, updateEquipment, deleteEquipment, getEquipmentById functions
Persist to AsyncStorage under @pearl_equipment with Date-safe serialization
Export EquipmentProvider and useEquipment hook
Add to src/context/index.ts barrel exports
Add EquipmentProvider to App.tsx provider chain (before DeviceProvider)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow same pattern as UtilityBillContext: createContext, Provider with AsyncStorage, isLoaded gate, useCallback mutators. EquipmentItem type already exported from components/EquipmentList.tsx.

userStories.title: Wire EquipmentEntryScreen to save real data
userStories.description: As a user, I want my equipment entries to actually save so I can see them later.
acceptanceCriteria: EquipmentEntryScreen calls useEquipment().addEquipment on save (new) or updateEquipment (edit)
Generates a unique ID for new equipment (equip_{timestamp})
Calculates status using existing calculateEquipmentStatus function from components
Sets estimatedLifespan from getTypicalLifespan if user doesn't specify
Sets typeLabel based on equipment type selection
After save, calls onSave callback to navigate back
When equipmentId prop is provided, loads existing equipment from context for editing
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: EquipmentEntryScreen already has a form with EquipmentFormData type. Wire onSave to build an EquipmentItem from form data and add to context. calculateEquipmentStatus and getTypicalLifespan already exported from components.

userStories.title: Wire MyHomeScreen to display real equipment from context
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my actual saved equipment on the My Home screen instead of mock data.
acceptanceCriteria: MyHomeScreen reads equipment from useEquipment() instead of MOCK_EQUIPMENT constant
Remove MOCK_EQUIPMENT hardcoded array from MyHomeScreen
Empty state shown when no equipment added (EquipmentList component handles this)
Equipment count updates dynamically based on context data
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Simple swap: replace MOCK_EQUIPMENT with useEquipment().equipment. EquipmentList already handles empty state. Import useEquipment from context barrel.

userStories.title: Wire EquipmentDetailScreen to read from context
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see real data on the equipment detail screen with working edit and delete.
acceptanceCriteria: EquipmentDetailScreen reads equipment from useEquipment().getEquipmentById instead of mock lookup or prop fallback
Remove any MOCK_EQUIPMENT_DATA hardcoded data from EquipmentDetailScreen
Delete button calls useEquipment().deleteEquipment and navigates back via onBack callback
Delete shows confirmation dialog with equipment name before executing
Edit button navigates to EquipmentEntry with equipmentId param (already wired in RootNavigator)
Shows 'Equipment not found' error state if ID doesn't match any record, with back button
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: EquipmentDetailScreen currently accepts equipment as prop. Change to read from context by ID. The navigation wrapper already passes equipmentId. Keep equipment prop as optional fallback during transition.

userStories.title: Replacement cost estimates
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see estimated replacement costs for my aging equipment so I can budget ahead.
acceptanceCriteria: Add REPLACEMENT_COST_ESTIMATES constant: Record<EquipmentType, { low: number; high: number; label: string }>
Values: hvac $8000-15000, waterHeater $1200-3000, insulation $1500-5000, windows $5000-15000, solar $15000-30000, appliance $500-3000, lighting $200-1000, other $500-5000
Add cost estimate card to EquipmentDetailScreen when status is 'aging' or 'replaceSoon'
Card shows range formatted with commas (e.g. '$8,000 – $15,000') with 'Typical replacement range' label
Card only visible to premium users (check @pearl_is_premium)
Export REPLACEMENT_COST_ESTIMATES from components barrel
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Add REPLACEMENT_COST_ESTIMATES to EquipmentList.tsx alongside existing constants. Cost card in detail screen uses same Card component with colors.warning or colors.hurting background tint.

userStories.title: Equipment aging notification trigger
userStories.description: As a user, I want to receive push notifications when my equipment is approaching end-of-life.
acceptanceCriteria: Create checkEquipmentAging function in src/services/EquipmentAgingService.ts
Reads equipment from AsyncStorage @pearl_equipment
For 'aging' equipment: generates notification 'Your {name} is {age} years old. Typical lifespan is {lifespan} years.'
For 'replaceSoon' equipment: generates notification 'Your {name} may need replacement soon. Check available rebates.'
Dedup: one notification per equipment per 30 days via @pearl_equipment_aging_notified AsyncStorage key
Add to services barrel exports
Call from BackgroundSyncService task alongside maintenance/seasonal checks
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow pattern of SeasonalTipService: read data, evaluate conditions, schedule local notification via expo-notifications, track last-notified timestamps. Notification category maps to existing equipment_aging category.

userStories.title: Equipment lifecycle insights in InsightEngine
userStories.description: As a user, I want my equipment status to generate insights on the dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Add generateEquipmentInsights(equipment: EquipmentItem[]) function to InsightEngine
'replaceSoon' equipment generates 'action' severity insight with title like 'Time to plan a {type} replacement'
'aging' equipment generates 'attention' severity insight with title like 'Your {name} is getting older'
Each insight includes suggested actions: check rebates (type: 'rebate'), find Pearl Pro (type: 'pearl_pro')
Insights map to 'operations' pillar for HVAC/waterHeater, 'energy' for solar/lighting, 'comfort' for insulation/windows
Export from services barrel
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow pattern of generateBillInsights in InsightEngine. Insight interface already exists with id, title, body, severity, pillar, suggestedActions. Equipment type → pillar mapping needed.

userStories.title: Premium gate for lifecycle features
userStories.description: As a free-tier user, I want to see basic equipment info but lifecycle tracking details are gated.
acceptanceCriteria: EquipmentDetailScreen checks @pearl_is_premium flag via AsyncStorage
When not premium: hide lifespan progress bar, replacement cost card, and rebate cards
Show 'Upgrade for lifecycle tracking' banner in their place listing locked features
Banner tap shows PremiumGate overlay (reuse existing component)
Equipment list and basic info (name, type, brand, status badge) always visible on free tier
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PremiumGate component already exists. Read @pearl_is_premium from AsyncStorage (default true for demo). Gate only the lifecycle-specific sections in EquipmentDetailScreen, not the entire screen.
